requirement: Authoritative product gate evidence index is stored under the product repository .git directory, not committed into product source by default.
The evidence index uses a fixed TSV column order so dashboard, gates, and tests parse it without prose.
Each evidence item has a stable source id that dashboard and gates can reference without parsing prose.
Source ids use a fixed namespace so product-operation blockers are stable.
Each evidence item records the workflow context such as free-development, product-improvement, or external-integration.
Each evidence item records the checked product repository root in sanitized form.
Each evidence item binds the result to the product repository HEAD where applicable.
Each evidence item records when the observation was made.
Each evidence item records freshness expectations without hard-coding them in the UI.
Status uses a fixed vocabulary that can distinguish not-run and stale evidence from unknown state.
Freshness is explicit so dashboard can separate current evidence from old evidence.
Each evidence item records whether the workflow context requires it.
Each evidence item records whether the value is authoritative, manual_required, advisory, or not collected.
Each evidence item lists the policy, manifest, command, or file sources used to produce it.
Each evidence item lists blocking source ids when the status is blocked or unknown.
Each evidence item may provide a read-only command preview for manual follow-up.
Product document resolution uses canonical docs paths and blocks root-level duplicate product repository documents.
The `none` legacy_paths value is a sentinel that means no legacy path is accepted for that structure row.
Manifest and structure rows use fixed required-mode values.
Manifest and structure contexts use fixed values, with `all` applying to every product workflow context.
Manifest and structure product types use fixed values, with `all` applying to every product type.
Missing required rows become product-operation blockers.
Missing optional rows are visible but not blockers.
Missing contextual rows depend on whether the current context/product type requires the row.
Stage manifests declare lifecycle stages without stack-specific code branches.
Test manifests declare required and optional product checks without stack-specific code branches.
CI manifests bind product CI expectations to real workflow files and evidence sources.
Integration manifests declare external services and approval/security evidence.
Security manifests declare required security checks and evidence.
Dashboard manifests declare which product surfaces can appear in dashboard data.
Product manifests declare entrypoint, source, and test authorities without stack-specific code branches.
Product manifest path roles use a fixed vocabulary so dashboard and gates can summarize scaffold authority.
Dashboard data exposes product authority as structured source status, not as UI-invented judgment.
Dashboard data exposes product manifest coverage and missing required surfaces.
Dashboard data exposes latest evidence status and freshness for Git, docs, CI, security, approvals, and gates.
Dashboard data exposes product-operation blockers separately from lesson-only progress blockers.
